capability_questions.capability_name: Service Exists and in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a policy management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a policy management service?

questions.prompt: Is the policy management service reviewed frequently to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the policy management service reviewed frequently to measure its efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a reference policy framework that is the reference for all policies in the enterprise? For example, does the enterprise have clear visibility on who can access what?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a reference policy framework that is the reference for all policies in the enterprise? For example, does the enterprise have clear visibility on who can access what?

questions.prompt: Is the policy framework applicable enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the policy framework applicable enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Is the policy framework reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the policy framework reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Is the policy framework reused when creating granular functional policies (like data access, authentication, etc.)?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the policy framework reused when creating granular functional policies (like data access, authentication, etc.)?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have formalized documented KPIs and KRIs for the policy management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have formalized documented KPIs and KRIs for the policy management service?

questions.prompt: Are the KPIs/KRIs for policy management effective enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the KPIs/KRIs for policy management effective enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Are the KPIs/KRIs for policy management reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the KPIs/KRIs for policy management reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an enterprise-wide centralized rule management system? Or does the enterprise depend on technology-specific management platforms to edit rules? Alternatively, does the enterprise have a centralized policy that is utilized by multiple enforcement points?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have an enterprise-wide centralized rule management system? Or does the enterprise depend on technology-specific management platforms to edit rules? Alternatively, does the enterprise have a centralized policy that is utilized by multiple enforcement points?

questions.prompt: Does the rule platform support automation and optimization of rules? Alternatively, can the deployed software-defined policy be automated to provide dynamic changes in policy?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the rule platform support automation and optimization of rules? Alternatively, can the deployed software-defined policy be automated to provide dynamic changes in policy?

questions.prompt: Does the rule management system integrate with a central decision-maker in the network to make automated contextual rule changes? Alternatively, does the enterprise have the capability to deploy policies automatically via software based on a centralized decision-maker or other information sources like threat intelligence?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the rule management system integrate with a central decision-maker in the network to make automated contextual rule changes? Alternatively, does the enterprise have the capability to deploy policies automatically via software based on a centralized decision-maker or other information sources like threat intelligence?

questions.prompt: Is the rule management system and its configuration reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule management system and its configuration reviewed frequently?

capability_questions.capability_name: Rule provisioning process (creation / modification) as part of optimization

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a documented and formalized process for rule creation?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a documented and formalized process for rule creation?

questions.prompt: Is the rule creation process applicable enterprise-wide for all technologies?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule creation process applicable enterprise-wide for all technologies?

questions.prompt: Is the rule creation and optimization automated with the rule management system or the management system for the specific technology?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule creation and optimization automated with the rule management system or the management system for the specific technology?

questions.prompt: Is the rule creation and optimization process reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule creation and optimization process reviewed frequently?

capability_questions.capability_name: Rule De-provisioning process (deletion) as part of optimization

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a documented and formalized process for rule deletion?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a documented and formalized process for rule deletion?

questions.prompt: Is the rule deletion process applicable enterprise-wide for all technologies?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule deletion process applicable enterprise-wide for all technologies?

questions.prompt: Is the rule deletion and optimization automated with the rule management system or the management system for the specific technology?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule deletion and optimization automated with the rule management system or the management system for the specific technology?

questions.prompt: Is the rule deletion and optimization process reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the rule deletion and optimization process reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a central rule management and policy architecture team, or are the teams specific to the product that deploys the rules (siloed policy vs. common policy)?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a central rule management and policy architecture team, or are the teams specific to the product that deploys the rules (siloed policy vs. common policy)?

questions.prompt: Is the policy management team composition formalized and documented as part of the department hierarchy and maintaining separation of duties?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the policy management team composition formalized and documented as part of the department hierarchy and maintaining separation of duties?
questions.evaluation_focus: Formalized team composition and separation of duties

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have the capability to maintain and configure software-defined policy at all locations?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have the capability to maintain and configure software-defined policy at all locations?

questions.prompt: Is the centralized policy deployed programmatically to all enforcement points? Or is the software-defined policy implemented for a smaller scope?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the centralized policy deployed programmatically to all enforcement points? Or is the software-defined policy implemented for a smaller scope?

questions.prompt: Are software-defined policy deployments automated?
questions.intent: Evaluate are software-defined policy deployments automated?

questions.prompt: Are the automated policies validated frequently to measure the efficacy of the automation?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the automated policies validated frequently to measure the efficacy of the automation?

questions.prompt: Do the network devices in the enterprise deploy context-based policy vs. IP-based policies?
questions.intent: Evaluate do the network devices in the enterprise deploy context-based policy vs. IP-based policies?

questions.prompt: Are context-based policies deployed enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate are context-based policies deployed enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Are the context-based policies reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the context-based policies reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Do the edge/access devices in the enterprise deploy context-based policy vs. just user ID-based policies?
questions.intent: Evaluate do the edge/access devices in the enterprise deploy context-based policy vs. just user ID-based policies?

questions.prompt: Are uniform authentication policies deployed enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate are uniform authentication policies deployed enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Are the authentication policies reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the authentication policies reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise design authorization policies based on context, attributes, environment, and other triggers?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise design authorization policies based on context, attributes, environment, and other triggers?

questions.prompt: Are uniform authorization policies deployed enterprise-wide (does the subject get the same access when accessing the same enterprise resource from any location)?
questions.intent: Evaluate are uniform authorization policies deployed enterprise-wide (does the subject get the same access when accessing the same enterprise resource from any location)?

questions.prompt: Are the authorization policies reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the authorization policies reviewed frequently?

capability_questions.capability_name: Network access and control policies (includes Guest NAC policies)

questions.prompt: Do the network security devices in the enterprise deploy the same context-based policy vs. just user IP-based policies? For example, a WAF, a load balancer, and a firewall must deploy the same subject-to-object access; however, it will be implemented based on technology. The logical access control must be the same.
questions.intent: Evaluate do the network security devices in the enterprise deploy the same context-based policy vs. just user IP-based policies? For example, a WAF, a load balancer, and a firewall must deploy the same subject-to-object access; however, it will be implemented based on technology. The logical access control must be the same.

questions.prompt: Are network security policies deployed enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate are network security policies deployed enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Are the network security policies reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the network security policies reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Do the applications in the enterprise deploy context-based policy vs. just user ID-based policies in addition to the network access control?
questions.intent: Evaluate do the applications in the enterprise deploy context-based policy vs. just user ID-based policies in addition to the network access control?

questions.prompt: Are uniform application access policies deployed enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate are uniform application access policies deployed enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Are the application access policies reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the application access policies reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Is data access in the enterprise controlled based on classification, context, environment, and other criteria?
questions.intent: Evaluate is data access in the enterprise controlled based on classification, context, environment, and other criteria?

questions.prompt: Are uniform data access policies deployed enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate are uniform data access policies deployed enterprise-wide?

questions.prompt: Are the data access policies reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the data access policies reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging rule and policy updates to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there a formal documented process for logging rule and policy updates to the SOC?

questions.prompt: Is logging of rule changes and lifecycle made securely to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate is logging of rule changes and lifecycle made securely to the SOC?

questions.prompt: Are dynamic changes to the rules captured and transferred to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate are dynamic changes to the rules captured and transferred to the SOC?
